title: hosta japonica var. tardiflora
intro: Hosta japonica var. tardiflora, also known as Tardiflora hosta, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Asparagaceae family and is native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Hosta japonica var. tardiflora include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or combining them with other herbs to create topical applications.
